Title: HrTCP20 dramatically enhance drought tolerance of sea buckthorn (Hippophae rhamnoides L.). by mediating the JA signaling pathway

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: HrTCP20 encodes a 299-amino-acid protein with a conserved TCP basic helix-loop-helix domain and clusters within the PCF subfamily of TCP transcription factors; overexpression effects on drought tolerance were demonstrated in heterologous Arabidopsis, whereas loss-of-function phenotypes were validated in the native sea buckthorn background.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. HrTCP20 is a TCP transcription factor from sea buckthorn encoding a 299-amino-acid protein with a conserved bHLH TCP domain and is strongly up-regulated (~18-fold) by PEG-induced drought stress.
2. Overexpression of HrTCP20 in Arabidopsis thaliana enhances drought tolerance, increasing SOD and POD activities and chlorophyll content while reducing MDA levels under drought.
3. VIGS-mediated silencing of HrTCP20 in sea buckthorn reduces drought tolerance, with decreased defense-related enzymes and RWC, increased REC, MDA, and H2O2, and more severe wilting and ROS accumulation under drought.
4. Silencing HrTCP20 significantly decreases JA content and specifically down-regulates the JA biosynthetic gene HrLOX2; yeast two-hybrid assays confirm a direct HrTCP20–HrLOX2 interaction, indicating that HrTCP20 improves drought resistance by modulating JA biosynthesis via HrLOX2.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Sea buckthorn RNA-seq dataset SRP121096 used to obtain HrTCP20 and JA pathway gene sequences.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of drought tolerance that enhances antioxidant enzyme activities and chlorophyll content, maintains higher water status, and reduces membrane damage and ROS accumulation under drought stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Silencing HrTCP20 decreases expression of the JA biosynthetic gene HrLOX2 and reduces JA accumulation under drought, while yeast two-hybrid assays show a direct HrTCP20–HrLOX2 protein interaction, indicating HrTCP20 positively regulates JA biosynthesis through HrLOX2 to modulate antioxidant defenses and drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Functions in the jasmonic acid (JA) signaling and biosynthetic pathway that mediates sea buckthorn responses to drought stress.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Regulates the jasmonic acid (JA) biosynthetic/signaling pathway indirectly via modulation of downstream JA biosynthetic gene HrLOX2.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: HrTCP20 -->(activation)--> HrLOX2: silencing HrTCP20 reduces HrLOX2 transcript levels and JA content under drought, and HrTCP20 protein physically interacts with HrLOX2, indicating positive regulation of this JA biosynthetic gene.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TCP transcription factor of the PCF (class I) subfamily containing a conserved basic helix-loop-helix TCP domain.
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: HrLOX2 (interaction demonstrated by yeast two-hybrid assay).
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Strongly induced by PEG-induced drought stress in sea buckthorn leaves, with transcript levels increasing continuously and reaching approximately 18-fold at 12 h of treatment.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: PEG-6000-induced drought stress and water depletion treatments applied to Arabidopsis and sea buckthorn; VIGS treatment in field-like conditions (~25 °C, 60–70% humidity).
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Drought stress tolerance and jasmonic acid-mediated signaling in forest tree species.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In Arabidopsis overexpression lines under 72 h drought, SOD and POD activities increased to approximately 1.37- and 1.35-fold of WT levels, respectively, while MDA content decreased to about 0.51-fold and chlorophyll (SPAD) content was higher than in WT. In HrTCP20-silenced sea buckthorn under drought, REC increased ~2.3-fold and RWC decreased by about 63.67% relative to control; MDA increased by ~0.47-fold, whereas POD, PRO, and SOD decreased by ~0.43-, 0.36-, and 0.58-fold, CAT decreased by ~2.1-fold, and H2O2 levels were ~0.4-fold higher than control.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Silencing HrTCP20 in sea buckthorn led to faster stomatal closure under drought, elevated H2O2 accumulation and stronger DAB staining, and increased membrane permeability (higher REC).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: 2–3-year-old sea buckthorn plants (F2 clone from H. rhamnoides subsp. mongolica × H. rhamnoides subsp. sinensis); Arabidopsis thaliana ecotype Columbia wild-type and HrTCP20-overexpression lines (OE-1, OE-2, OE-3); VIGS-treated sea buckthorn branches (TRV:HrTCP20, TRV:HrPDS, TRV:00).
Plant_Genes.Variety: F2 clone from cross of Hippophae rhamnoides subsp. mongolica × Hippophae rhamnoides L. subsp. sinensis
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Gene cloning from RNA-seq-derived sequence; RT-PCR and sequencing; generation of 35S:HrTCP20 overexpression vector and Arabidopsis transgenic lines; virus-induced gene silencing (TRV:HrTCP20) in sea buckthorn; PEG-6000 or water-depletion drought treatments; measurement of SOD, POD, CAT, MDA, PRO, chlorophyll (SPAD); RWC and REC determination; ROS detection by DAB staining and H2O2 quantification; hormone (IAA, SA, JA, ABA) measurement by HPLC-MS/MS; qRT-PCR for gene expression; yeast two-hybrid assay for HrTCP20–HrLOX2 interaction; statistical analysis by t-test.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in Arabidopsis thaliana (35S:HrTCP20) followed by PEG-induced drought assays and physiological measurements; VIGS-mediated silencing in sea buckthorn with drought treatment; qRT-PCR expression analysis; hormone quantification; yeast two-hybrid interaction assay with HrLOX2.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified from a sea buckthorn RNA-seq database (accession SRP121096) and cloned from cDNA by PCR amplification followed by sequencing and multiple sequence alignment with TCP20 homologs from other species.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Provides a validated drought-resistance transcription factor in sea buckthorn that enhances antioxidant defenses, maintains water status, and acts through JA signaling, making HrTCP20 a promising target gene for molecular breeding and genetic engineering of drought-tolerant forest trees.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: HrLOX2 is one of three LOX classes (LOX1, LOX2, LOX3) examined; only HrLOX2 expression was markedly reduced in HrTCP20-silenced plants, highlighting its specific linkage to HrTCP20-mediated JA biosynthesis under drought.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. HrLOX2 was identified as a lipoxygenase gene involved in the JA biosynthetic pathway in sea buckthorn.
2. Among several JA biosynthesis-related genes tested, HrLOX2 expression is the most strongly and specifically reduced (~0.36-fold of control) in HrTCP20-silenced plants under drought stress.
3. Reduced HrLOX2 expression in HrTCP20-silenced plants coincides with a significant decrease in JA content, linking HrLOX2 to HrTCP20-dependent JA biosynthesis under drought.
4. Yeast two-hybrid assays demonstrate a direct physical interaction between HrLOX2 and HrTCP20 proteins, supporting a model in which HrTCP20 modulates JA biosynthesis and drought response through HrLOX2.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Sea buckthorn RNA-seq dataset SRP121096 used to identify HrLOX2 and other JA pathway gene sequences.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions as a JA biosynthetic gene whose expression is specifically and strongly down-regulated when HrTCP20 is silenced, contributing to reduced JA accumulation under drought stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Acts as a JA biosynthetic enzyme whose transcript abundance is positively regulated by the transcription factor HrTCP20; reduced HrLOX2 expression in HrTCP20-silenced plants is associated with decreased JA content, and direct physical interaction between HrLOX2 and HrTCP20 proteins is detected by yeast two-hybrid.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Participates in the jasmonic acid biosynthetic pathway that contributes to drought-responsive hormone signaling in sea buckthorn.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Jasmonic acid biosynthetic pathway, at the 13-lipoxygenase (LOX2) step converting α-linolenic acid to 13-hydroperoxyoctadecatrienoic acid (13-HPOT).
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: HrTCP20 -->(activation)--> HrLOX2: HrLOX2 transcript levels are significantly decreased when HrTCP20 is silenced, and HrLOX2 physically interacts with HrTCP20, placing HrLOX2 downstream of HrTCP20 in the JA biosynthetic pathway that influences drought responses.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Lipoxygenase (LOX2) class enzyme implicated in 13-lipoxygenase activity within the JA biosynthetic pathway.
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: HrTCP20 (direct protein–protein interaction shown by yeast two-hybrid).
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Among JA biosynthesis-related genes, HrLOX2 shows the strongest response to HrTCP20 silencing, with its expression significantly reduced (~0.36-fold relative to control) in VIGS-treated plants under drought stress.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: PEG-6000-induced drought stress applied to VIGS-treated and control sea buckthorn plants.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: JA biosynthesis and hormone-mediated drought response in sea buckthorn.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress – drought response via JA biosynthesis.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In HrTCP20-silenced sea buckthorn under drought, HrLOX2 expression was reduced to approximately 0.36-fold of control levels, coinciding with a ~1.23-fold reduction in JA content compared with control plants.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Sea buckthorn plants subjected to VIGS (TRV:HrTCP20, TRV:00) and PEG-6000 drought treatment; sequences of HrLOX2 obtained from sea buckthorn RNA-seq data and NCBI for cloning and vector construction.
Plant_Genes.Variety: F2 clone from cross of Hippophae rhamnoides subsp. mongolica × Hippophae rhamnoides L. subsp. sinensis
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Derivation of HrLOX2 sequence from RNA-seq and NCBI; cloning and primer design; qRT-PCR expression profiling of JA biosynthesis genes (HrAOS, HrAOC, HrLOX1, HrLOX2, HrLOX3) in VIGS-treated and control plants; PEG-6000 drought treatment; hormone quantification; construction of pGAD-HrTCP20 and pGBT-HrLOX2 vectors; yeast two-hybrid assays to test HrTCP20–HrLOX2 protein interaction.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR analysis of HrLOX2 expression in HrTCP20-silenced versus control sea buckthorn under drought; yeast two-hybrid assay demonstrating physical interaction between HrLOX2 protein and HrTCP20.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a putative LOX2-class lipoxygenase from a sea buckthorn RNA-seq database (SRP121096) and from NCBI enzyme-encoding gene sequences, then cloned for qRT-PCR and yeast two-hybrid experiments.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: As a key JA biosynthetic lipoxygenase whose expression is positively regulated by HrTCP20 and correlates with JA content under drought, HrLOX2 represents a downstream target whose manipulation, together with HrTCP20, could be used to fine-tune JA levels and improve drought response in sea buckthorn breeding and genetic engineering programs.
